title: bentinckia renda
intro: Bentinckia renda, also known as the Toddy Palm or Palm Toddy, is a species of palm tree native to the Western Ghats of India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Bentinckia renda include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The herbal preparations of the plant involve extracting its sap, which is then used to make medicines, or its leaves and bark, which are used to create teas and infusions.
